codeReview.summary: Comprehensive Zod-based request validation implementation that fully satisfies all requirements. The feature adds a complete schema.ts with validation for POST /spikes (required page/type, enum validation, email/URL/UUID validation) and POST /shares metadata (webhook_url HTTPS + private IP rejection). Standardized error responses follow the specified shape { error: string, code: string, details?: array }. All 26 validation tests pass.

issues.description: createSpikeSchema allows both 'project' and 'projectKey' as optional fields with no validation that at least one is provided. This is backward-compatible but could allow requests with neither field. Feature specification didn't require this validation, but it may be worth adding in future.

The private IP detection for webhook URLs doesn't cover all private/reserved ranges (e.g., 100.64.0.0/10 CGNAT, fc00::/7 IPv6). Current implementation covers documented requirements (10.x, 172.16-31.x, 192.168.x, 169.254.x, .local, .internal) which is sufficient for the feature spec.

sharedStateObservations.observation: The project lacks an AGENTS.md file in the worker directory to document worker-specific coding patterns (e.g., Zod schema organization, error code naming conventions, validation patterns). The worker follows good practices (centralized schema.ts, standardized error utilities) but these aren't documented for future workers.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: worker/src/schema.ts defines ErrorCodes enum and validation patterns; worker/src/utils/response.ts exports validationErrorResponse() - these patterns should be documented for consistency across future features.

sharedStateObservations.observation: No new services or commands needed for this feature - it's a code-only change that integrates with existing test infrastructure.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: Feature only adds Zod dependency and validation logic; tests run via existing npm test command.

summary: PASS: The request-validation-zod feature is fully implemented and meets all requirements. The worker now validates POST /spikes and POST /shares requests using Zod schemas before business logic execution. Error responses follow the standardized shape with error, code, and optional details fields. Webhook URLs are validated to be HTTPS and not point to private IPs. All 26 validation tests pass. Two minor non-blocking observations noted: (1) schema allows neither project nor projectKey which is backward-compatible but may want stricter validation in future, (2) private IP detection covers spec requirements but could be extended for completeness. No shared state gaps requiring immediate action.
